So they swore to each other: لَنُبَيِّتَنَّهُۥ وَأَهْلَهُۥ – "We will surely attack him and his family by night." The word لَنُبَيِّتَنَّهُۥ specifically means a nighttime raid, a surprise attack under cover of darkness. They're planning to assassinate Salih (AS) and kill his entire family as collateral damage.

Why all nine groups together? Why not just one tribe handle it? Because if one group did it alone, tribal warfare could erupt. The victim's tribe would demand revenge against the perpetrators' tribe. But if members from all nine groups participate, it diffuses the responsibility. It's a united action, so no single tribe can be blamed and targeted for retaliation. This is strategic, calculated evil.

ثُمَّ لَنَقُولَنَّ لِوَلِيِّهِۦ مَا شَهِدْنَا مَهْلِكَ أَهْلِهِۦ – "Then we will surely say to his guardian tribe: We did not witness the destruction of his family." The word وَلِىّ refers to his guardian, his protector, his tribal support. They're planning their cover story: "We didn't see anything. We have no idea what happened. Everybody was asleep. It's tragic, but we didn't witness it."

The word مَهْلِك encompasses both the time and place of destruction – we didn't see when it happened, we don't know where it happened. Complete denial.

وَإِنَّا لَصَـٰدِقُونَ – "And we're telling the truth. We'll testify in court if needed. We had nothing to do with it."

This is covert assassination, collateral damage accepted, cover story prepared. Does this sound familiar? This is exactly what was being planned against the Prophet ﷺ in Makkah. The Quraysh held secret meetings and plotted to kill him in a way that would diffuse blame across multiple tribes. The Quran is giving the Prophet ﷺ intelligence briefings disguised as historical narratives: "This is what they're planning. This is what they've already decided in their secret meetings. This is the level of conspiracy you're facing."

Corrupt governments and organizations use these tactics to this day – clandestine operations, plausible deniability, coordinated lies. "If you get caught, we'll deny all connection to you."

translation: And they planned a plan, and We planned a plan, while they perceived not
commentary:
وَمَكَرُوا۟ مَكْرًا وَمَكَرْنَا مَكْرًا وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ

وَمَكَرُوا۟ مَكْرًا – "And they planned a plan" or "they plotted a plot." They made their scheme, they thought they had it all figured out, they coordinated their conspiracy down to the last detail.

وَمَكَرْنَا مَكْرًا – "And We planned a plan." When Allah ﷻ uses مَكْر about Himself, it means He countered their scheme with a superior plan, He outmaneuvered them. They thought they were so clever, but Allah ﷻ had His own plan in motion that would completely neutralize theirs.

وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ – "while they had no awareness." They had no idea what was coming. They didn't sense Allah's plan working against them. They thought they were in control, that their conspiracy would succeed. They were completely blind to the reality that Allah ﷻ was already several steps ahead of them.

translation: Then look how was the outcome of their plan - that We destroyed them and their people, all
commentary:
فَٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ مَكْرِهِمْ أَنَّا دَمَّرْنَـٰهُمْ وَقَوْمَهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ

فَٱنظُرْ – "So look! Observe! Take a good look!" This is directed to the Prophet ﷺ and to us. Don't just read this as ancient history – examine it carefully and see the pattern.

كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ مَكْرِهِمْ – "How was the outcome of their plotting?" What happened to their brilliant scheme, their foolproof conspiracy?

أَنَّا دَمَّرْنَـٰهُمْ وَقَوْمَهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ – "We destroyed them and their people all together." The word دَمَّرْنَا doesn't just mean destroyed – it means utterly crushed, completely annihilated. The emphasis is on total destruction.

Those few leaders who held that secret meeting, who swore oaths to each other, who planned the assassination – they were specifically destroyed. And then the entire disbelieving nation that supported or tolerated their corruption was destroyed as well. Nobody was spared. Their plotting led not to Salih's death but to their own complete obliteration.

translation: So those are their houses, desolate because of the wrong they had done. Indeed in that is a sign for people who know
commentary:
فَتِلْكَ بُيُوتُهُمْ خَاوِيَةًۢ بِمَا ظَلَمُوٓا۟ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً لِّقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ

فَتِلْكَ بُيُوتُهُمْ خَاوِيَةً – "And there are their houses, collapsed" or "turned upside down." The word خَاوِيَة means empty, desolate, collapsed in on itself. The Arabs traveling through that region could still see the ruins of Thamud. The Quran isn't talking about some mythical people in a faraway land – the evidence was right there. "Look over there. You can see their destroyed homes. You pass by them on your trade routes."

بِمَا ظَلَمُوٓا۟

translation: And We saved those who believed and used to fear Allah
And [mention] Lot, when he said to his people, "Do you commit immorality while you are seeing
Do you indeed approach men with desire instead of women? Rather, you are a people behaving ignorantly
But the answer of his people was not except that they said, "Expel the family of Lot from your city. Indeed, they are people who keep themselves pure
So We saved him and his family, except for his wife; We destined her to be of those who remained behind
And We rained upon them a rain [of stones], and evil was the rain of those who were warned
Say, [O Muhammad], "Praise be to Allah, and peace upon His servants whom He has chosen. Is Allah better or what they associate with Him

[More precisely], is He [not best] who created the heavens and the earth and sent down for you rain from the sky, causing to grow thereby gardens of joyful beauty which you could not [otherwise] have grown the trees thereof? Is there a deity with Allah? [No], but they are a people who ascribe equals [to Him]

Is He [not best] who made the earth a stable ground and placed within it rivers and made for it firmly set mountains and placed between the two seas a barrier? Is there a deity with Allah? [No], but most of them do not know
Is He [not best] who responds to the desperate one when he calls upon Him and removes evil and makes you inheritors of the earth? Is there a deity with Allah? Little do you remember
commentary:
أَمَّن يُجِيبُ ٱلْمُضْطَرَّ إِذَا دَعَاهُ وَيَكْشِفُ ٱلسُّوٓءَ وَيَجْعَلُكُمْ خُلَفَآءَ ٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ أَءِلَـٰهٌ مَّعَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ قَلِيلًا مَّا تَذَكَّرُونَ

This ayah presents a powerful rhetorical question that cuts to the heart of human experience. Who responds to the one in desperate straits when they call out? The word المضطر describes someone forced into difficulty, someone stuck in tremendous trouble with nowhere to turn. When that person calls on Allah ﷻ, He responds immediately. The verb يُجِيبُ carries this sense of immediate response, not a delayed or conditional answer.

Allah ﷻ then describes two more manifestations of His power: He removes harm and evil from our lives, and He makes us successive generations on the earth - خُلَفَآءَ ٱلْأَرْضِ. Generation after generation, humanity continues because Allah ﷻ maintains this system.

The ayah concludes with the piercing question: أَءِلَـٰهٌ مَّعَ ٱللَّهِ - is there any god, any entity worthy of worship and obedience, other than Allah? The final statement قَلِيلًا مَّا تَذَكَّرُونَ emphasizes how little effort people make to remember these truths, how little they take real advice to heart.

translation: Is He [not best] who guides you through the darknesses of the land and sea and who sends the winds as good tidings before His mercy? Is there a deity with Allah? High is Allah above whatever they associate with Him
commentary:
أَمَّن يَهْدِيكُمْ فِى ظُلُمَـٰتِ ٱلْبَرِّ وَٱلْبَحْرِ وَمَن يُرْسِلُ ٱلرِّيَـٰحَ بُشْرًۢا بَيْنَ يَدَىْ رَحْمَتِهِۦٓ ۗ أَءِلَـٰهٌ مَّعَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ تَعَـٰلَى ٱللَّهُ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ

The ayah continues the rhetorical pattern, now focusing on guidance through darkness. Who guides you through the darkness of land and sea? When traveling through the middle of the desert, your only guidance comes from the stars Allah ﷻ placed in the sky. When crossing the ocean, again Allah's creation provides direction.

Then Allah ﷻ describes how He sends winds full of good news بُشْرًۢا right ahead of His mercy. The phrase بَيْنَ يَدَىْ رَحْمَتِهِۦٓ - literally "between the hands of His mercy" - means right before His mercy arrives. The rain is coming, but before it arrives, the winds come first. When farmers sense that weather change, when they feel those winds, they begin to celebrate. The winds themselves become a means of celebration and good news.

Again comes the question: أَءِلَـٰهٌ مَّعَ ٱللَّهِ - is there any god besides Allah? But this time the response is different: تَعَـٰلَى ٱللَّهُ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ - how exalted, how high, how elevated Allah ﷻ is above the kinds of shirk they commit, above the partnerships and associations they make with Him.

translation: Is He [not best] who begins creation and then repeats it and who provides for you from the heaven and earth? Is there a deity with Allah? Say, "Produce your proof, if you should be truthful
commentary:
أَمَّن يَبْدَؤُا۟ ٱلْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُۥ وَمَن يَرْزُقُكُم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ أَءِلَـٰهٌ مَّعَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ قُلْ هَاتُوا۟ بُرْهَـٰنَكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ

This ayah addresses the fundamental question of creation and resurrection. Who started creation from nothing, and who will bring it back? Allah ﷻ created you the first time, then He will take you back - you'll return to the earth just as you were once inside your mother's womb. The earth becomes like a mother, and she will deliver you again at resurrection.

Then comes the question of provision: Who provides for you from the sky and the earth - the rain from above and the vegetation that grows below?

Once more: أَءِلَـٰهٌ مَّعَ ٱللَّهِ - is there any god besides Allah? But now Allah ﷻ commands His Messenger ﷺ with stronger language: قُلْ هَاتُوا۟ بُرْهَـٰنَكُمْ - tell them to bring their evidence. The word هَاتُوا۟ isn't just "bring" - it's urgent, almost angry: "Hurry up and bring your proof!" إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ - if you've really been telling the truth.

translation: Say, "None in the heavens and earth knows the unseen except Allah, and they do not perceive when they will be resurrected
commentary:
قُل لَّا يَعْلَمُ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ٱلْغَيْبَ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ ۚ وَمَا يَشْعُرُونَ أَيَّانَ يُبْعَثُونَ

Tell them: no one in the heavens or the earth knows the unseen except Allah ﷻ. This is an absolute statement - whoever exists in the skies and whoever walks on earth, none of them possess knowledge of the غَيْبَ, the unseen realm.

The ayah continues with a chilling observation: وَمَا يَشْعُرُونَ أَيَّانَ يُبْعَثُونَ - they have no realization, no feeling whatsoever about when they will be raised from the dead. They walk through life completely unaware of when that moment will come.

translation: Rather, their knowledge is arrested concerning the Hereafter. Rather, they are in doubt about it. Rather, they are, concerning it, blind
commentary:
بَلِ ٱدَّٰرَكَ عِلْمُهُمْ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۚ بَلْ هُمْ فِى شَكٍّ مِّنْهَا ۖ بَلْ هُم مِّنْهَا عَمُونَ

This ayah introduces something new and remarkable about the disbelievers' knowledge. بَلِ ٱدَّٰرَكَ عِلْمُهُمْ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ - their knowledge of the hereafter has reached the depths. The word ادَّارَكَ comes from the root meaning to tie one rope to another to another, because a single rope isn't long enough to reach the bottom of the ocean. When you keep tying ropes together to reach the very bottom, that's تَدَارُك - getting to the depths of something.

What does this mean? Little by little, piece by piece, knowledge about the akhirah has kept coming to them. They've heard about it repeatedly. This isn't the first time they're learning about resurrection. Their own ancestors were told about the akhirah by their prophets - the teachings of Ibrahim (AS) and Isma'il (AS) have made their way down through the generations.

Yet despite this accumulated knowledge: بَلْ هُمْ فِى شَكٍّ مِّنْهَا - they remain in doubt about it. Even worse: بَلْ هُم مِّنْهَا عَمُونَ - when it comes to this subject, they choose to be blind. عَمُونَ suggests willful blindness, deliberately closing their eyes to the truth. As Allah ﷻ says elsewhere, it's not the eyes that become blind, but the hearts in the chests.

translation: And those who disbelieve say, "When we have become dust as well as our forefathers, will we indeed be brought out [of the graves]
commentary:
وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ أَءِذَا كُنَّا تُرَٰبًا وَءَابَآؤُنَآ أَئِنَّا لَمُخْرَجُونَ

Now we hear the voice of the disbelievers directly. They say: when we've been reduced to dust, when our bodies have completely decayed, and our ancestors have turned into even more dirt than we have - if you open their graves you won't find anything but dust, no remnants remain - أَئِنَّا لَمُخْرَجُونَ - will we really be brought out again?

translation: We have been promised this, we and our forefathers, before. This is not but legends of the former peoples
commentary:
لَقَدْ وُعِدْنَا هَـٰذَا نَحْنُ وَءَابَآؤُنَا مِن قَبْلُ إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّآ أَسَـٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ

They continue their objection: we've been promised this before. We've heard this, and our ancestors heard this too. This connects back to the previous ayah where Allah ﷻ said their knowledge had reached the depths - they remember the teachings that came down through their lineage. They remember the warnings of Ibrahim (AS) and Isma'il (AS), but they dismiss them.

إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّآ أَسَـٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ - these are just legends, stories from ancient times. The word أَسَـٰطِيرُ is important because it suggests these are tales told by unsophisticated people from earlier eras. Their attitude is: those older generations were simpler folks, less evolved, less advanced than we are. When you told them about an afterlife, they believed you because they were naive. But we're smarter than that now. We've moved past those old times.

translation: Say, [O Muhammad], "Travel through the land and observe how was the end of the criminals
commentary:
قُلْ سِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَٱنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ

The response to their arrogance is simple and devastating: قُلْ سِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ - tell them to go around in the land, to travel and explore. فَٱنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ - then take a deep look for yourselves. Really examine what was the outcome, what was the end result of the criminals who came before you.

The ruins are still there. The archaeological evidence of destroyed nations remains. Go see for yourself what happened to those who rejected their messengers.

translation: And grieve not over them or be in distress from what they conspire
commentary:
وَلَا تَحْزَنْ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا تَكُن فِى ضَيْقٍ مِّمَّا يَمْكُرُونَ

Now Allah ﷻ turns to comfort His Messenger ﷺ directly. وَلَا تَحْزَنْ عَلَيْهِمْ - don't grieve over them, don't worry about them. This echoes the beginning of Surah Ash-Shu'ara, where Allah ﷻ asked, "Perhaps you will kill yourself in grief over them?"

وَلَا تَكُن فِى ضَيْقٍ مِّمَّا يَمْكُرُونَ - and don't even be uncomfortable, don't feel tightness in your chest over the plots they're making. This is the second reference to plots in this surah. The first was about Salih (AS) - nine tribal leaders plotted to kill him and his family in the middle of the night. Allah ﷻ said they made their plot and He made His plot.

Now at the end of Salih's story, Allah ﷻ tells the Prophet ﷺ: don't worry about their schemes against you. The parallel is clear - plots were made against Salih (AS), and plots are being made against you. But just as Allah ﷻ protected Salih (AS), He will protect you.

translation: And they say, "When is [the fulfillment of] this promise, if you should be truthful
commentary:
وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ هَـٰذَا ٱلْوَعْدُ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ

The disbelievers mock: مَتَىٰ هَـٰذَا ٱلْوَعْدُ - when is this promise coming? They're referring to two things: when will this nation be punished, and when is Judgment Day itself?

There's a critical difference between Quraysh and all the other nations described in the Quran. Those previous nations were destroyed by what we would call natural disasters - floods, fire, something from the sky, angels coming to annihilate them, homes turned upside down. Did that happen to Makkah? No, it didn't.

We have hindsight now. Looking back at history, we see it didn't happen that way. But punishment did come. The law of divine punishment will be maintained - a nation that rejects its messenger will face consequences. How will it happen? In previous cases, it was through angels, floods, earthquakes, winds. In the case of Makkah, the delivery mechanism will be different: the Companions themselves will become the means of punishment.

This is what sets Makkah apart. The punishment will come, but it will come in installments. As Allah ﷻ says in another place, "We will make them taste a lesser punishment before the greater punishment." The first installation will be the Battle of Badr. Then Uhud. Eventually, the conquest of Makkah itself. Just as nine signs were given to Musa (AS) one after another until Pharaoh was finally destroyed, the punishment here will come in stages.

When they ask "When is this promise coming?" they're really saying: we haven't seen any consequences for rejecting you, Muhammad ﷺ. Nothing's happened. We haven't changed. You haven't even seen the temperature shift. We're still in power, you're still the ones being laughed at and beaten up. So where's this divine punishment you keep talking about?

translation: Say, "Perhaps it is close behind you - some of that for which you are impatient
commentary:
قُلْ عَسَىٰٓ أَن يَكُونَ رَدِفَ لَكُم بَعْضُ ٱلَّذِى تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ

Allah's response: قُلْ عَسَىٰٓ أَن يَكُونَ رَدِفَ لَكُم - say to them: perhaps, very soon, it's right behind you. The word عَسَىٰٓ indicates high possibility - this isn't a distant maybe, this is something likely and imminent.

رَدِفَ is particularly powerful imagery. It refers to the person riding behind you on a horse or camel, the one who guards your back. When two riders travel together and one follows directly behind the other, turning when he turns, moving when he moves - that's the radif. The punishment they're asking about mockingly? It's already been released. It's riding right behind them. They just don't know it's going to catch up to them very soon.

بَعْضُ ٱلَّذِى تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ - some of what you're rushing toward, what you're demanding impatiently. Allah ﷻ is already alluding to Badr, already hinting at what's coming.

translation: And indeed, your Lord is full of bounty for the people, but most of them do not show gratitude
commentary:
وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى ٱلنَّاسِ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَشْكُرُونَ

Your Master, no doubt, possesses great favor for the people. Which people? Those the Quraysh currently mock and look down upon - the Muslims who seem to have no power right now, the weak and oppressed believers. Allah ﷻ intends to empower them. When will that favor be displayed? At Badr, and in what follows.

This connects back to earlier in the surah. Before discussing Prophet Salih (AS), we learned about him migrating from his nation, leaving them behind except for those who refused to believe. What's going to happen in the Prophet's ﷺ life? He's going to migrate too. He'll leave Makkah, and he's been told: leave in the middle of the night because they'll try to kill you. But you'll stay safe, your loved ones will be protected.

وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَشْكُرُونَ - yet most of them will not show gratitude. This also suggests that Allah ﷻ has already given tremendous favors to Makkah itself, and still they refuse to be thankful.

translation: And indeed, your Lord knows what their breasts conceal and what they declare
commentary:
وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَيَعْلَمُ مَا تُكِنُّ صُدُورُهُمْ وَمَا يُعْلِنُونَ

Your Master knows what their chests hide deep inside their vaults. The word تُكِنُّ comes from كِنّ, which refers to vaults that are impossible to penetrate, barriers you cannot cross through. The Quran itself is described as being فِى كِتَـٰبٍ مَّكْنُونٍ - in a book that is hidden away in a protected vault.

مَا تُكِنُّ صُدُورُهُمْ - what their chests keep buried deep inside. وَمَا يُعْلِنُونَ - and what they pronounce openly, what they show on the outside. Allah ﷻ knows the contradiction between the two.

Deep inside, they're impressed with the Quran. Outside, they mock and say, "When is the punishment coming?" Allah ﷻ knows the internal reality they work so hard to hide. They have to make an extraordinary effort to suppress that feeling, to keep their genuine impression of the Quran's power hidden. They go out of their way to insult the Quran, to put it down, all to suppress what Allah ﷻ knows is hiding deep within them. They have to actively work against their own hearts to maintain their public rejection.

translation: And there is nothing concealed within the heaven and the earth except that it is in a clear Register
commentary:
وَمَا مِنْ غَآئِبَةٍ فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ إِلَّا فِى كِتَـٰبٍ مُّبِينٍ

There is not a single hidden thing - غَآئِبَةٍ - in the sky or on the earth except that it exists in a clear, written record. This encompasses everything: atoms, every leaf, every drop of water. All of these fall within Allah's knowledge, and we understand that from other ayat.

But now this takes on deeper meaning after the previous ayah. Even the secrets inside a heart - things you cannot extract through surgery or any physical means - those intangible realities that don't belong to the physical world, those too are in Allah's possession. إِلَّا فِى كِتَـٰبٍ مُّبِينٍ - all of it exists in a clear, manifest book in Allah's knowledge.

translation: Indeed, this Qur'an relates to the Children of Israel most of that over which they disagree
commentary:
إِنَّ هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانَ يَقُصُّ عَلَىٰ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ أَكْثَرَ ٱلَّذِى هُمْ فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ

This Quran narrates to the Children of Israel much of what they disagree about. The Quran tells them things they themselves don't know with certainty about their own history. Who was Musa (AS)? What should their attitude be toward him? How should they respond to Yusuf (AS)? What were the actual miracles given to Musa (AS)?

There are so many details of their own legacy that they've twisted and distorted through the generations that they no longer know the straight version. The Quran came and corrected the record, telling them what actually happened with Musa (AS) and their own history. In fact, you'll see a case study of this principle in the very next surah, Surah Al-Qasas, where Allah ﷻ will present particular aspects of Jewish history that contradict their version - details they deny and disagree with, even though they're part of their own story.

translation: And indeed, it is guidance and mercy for the believers
commentary:
وَإِنَّهُۥ لَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةٌ لِّلْمُؤْمِنِينَ

No doubt about it - this Quran truly is guidance and mercy for all those who believe. Notice the shift: even the Children of Israel are not told, "You're an accursed nation." Rather, the message is: if you come to accept this as guidance, if you become believers, then it's guidance and mercy even for you. The door remains open for anyone who chooses to believe.

translation: Indeed, your Lord will judge between them by His [wise] judgement. And He is the Exalted in Might, the Knowing
commentary:
إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يَقْضِى بَيْنَهُم بِحُكْمِهِۦ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ

Your Master will pass His verdict among them بِحُكْمِهِۦ - by virtue of His judgment, His wisdom, His power. The word حُكْم connects to حِكْمَة it encompasses both the power to rule and the wisdom in that rule.

وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ - He is the Ultimate Authority, the All-Knowing. This is significant because at the beginning of the surah, Allah ﷻ mentioned two names: الْحَكِيمُ and الْعَلِيمُ. Then we saw الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ. Here at the end, Allah ﷻ maintains العَزِيز but takes the other attribute - العَلِيم - maintaining the sequence established at the beginning of the surah.

translation: So rely upon Allah; indeed, you are upon the clear truth
commentary:
فَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّكَ عَلَى ٱلْحَقِّ ٱلْمُبِينِ

Therefore, maintain your trust upon Allah. إِنَّكَ عَلَى ٱلْحَقِّ ٱلْمُبِينِ - you are committed to the clear truth. You stand upon manifest truth. You should never have any doubt. The only thing you need to rely on is Allah ﷻ, because what you stand for isn't subject to any legitimate criticism. What you're calling to isn't questionable.

translation: Indeed, you will not make the dead hear, nor will you make the deaf hear the call when they have turned their backs retreating
commentary:
إِنَّكَ لَا تُسْمِعُ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ وَلَا تُسْمِعُ ٱلصُّمَّ ٱلدُّعَآءَ إِذَا وَلَّوْا۟ مُدْبِرِينَ

The Prophet ﷺ might be thinking: if I'm on the clear truth, why doesn't anyone listen? Allah ﷻ responds: إِنَّكَ لَا تُسْمِعُ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ - you cannot make the dead listen. The Arabic uses one powerful verb تُسْمِعُ that combines two meanings in English: "to make" and "to hear" - to cause someone to listen.

وَلَا تُسْمِعُ ٱلصُّمَّ ٱلدُّعَآءَ - and you will not make the deaf hear the call. They don't have the ability to hear you anymore. إِذَا وَلَّوْا۟ مُدْبِرِينَ - especially when they turn away and show their backs, when they actively choose to walk away from the message.

translation: And you cannot guide the blind away from their error. You will only make hear those who believe in Our verses so they are Muslims [submitting to Allah]
commentary:
وَمَآ أَنتَ بِهَـٰدِى ٱلْعُمْىِ عَن ضَلَـٰلَتِهِمْ ۖ إِن تُسْمِعُ إِلَّا مَن يُؤْمِنُ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا فَهُم مُّسْلِمُونَ

You absolutely will not be guiding the blind away from their misguidance. العُمْىِ - those who are blind, and here it's plural, referring to those who choose spiritual blindness.

إِن تُسْمِعُ إِلَّا مَن يُؤْمِنُ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا - you will only have the ability to make those listen who believe in Our signs. فَهُم مُّسْلِمُونَ - then they are the ones who submit, who surrender themselves to the truth. Your message will reach those whose hearts are open to receiving it.

translation: And when the word befalls them, We will bring forth for them a creature from the earth speaking to them, [saying] that the people were, of Our verses, not certain [in faith]
commentary:
۞ وَإِذَا وَقَعَ ٱلْقَوْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ أَخْرَجْنَا لَهُمْ دَآبَّةً مِّنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ تُكَلِّمُهُمْ أَنَّ ٱلنَّاسَ كَانُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا لَا يُوقِنُونَ

When the word falls upon them - وَقَعَ suggests something dropping like an anvil or hammer, when the verdict drops on them - أَخْرَجْنَا لَهُمْ دَآبَّةً مِّنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ - We will extract for them a great creature from the earth.

تُكَلِّمُهُمْ - it will speak to them, a beast that will communicate. أَنَّ ٱلنَّاسَ كَانُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا لَا يُوقِنُونَ  - conveying that these people, they were the ones who had no conviction in Our signs.

This refers to دَآبَّةُ الأَرْض, mentioned in hadith literature as one of the great signs before Judgment Day. The authentic narrations describe this beast marking the disbelievers. The exact nature of this creature, how it will speak to people, and what it will say - these details remain among the متشابهات, the matters whose full reality Allah ﷻ knows best.

translation: And [warn of] the Day when We will gather from every nation a company of those who deny Our signs, and they will be [driven] in rows
commentary:
وَيَوْمَ نَحْشُرُ مِن كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ فَوْجًا مِّمَّن يُكَذِّبُ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا فَهُمْ يُوزَعُونَ

After the great signs comes what logically follows: Judgment Day itself. وَيَوْمَ نَحْشُرُ مِن كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ فَوْجًا - the day on which We gather from every single nation a huge legion, a massive group.

مِّمَّن يُكَذِّبُ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا - from among those who used to deny Our signs. فَهُمْ يُوزَعُونَ - then they will be stationed, set up, broken into organized battalions.

This echoes Sulayman's (AS) organized armies earlier in the surah. He had his forces arranged in disciplined rows. Now another parade, another march is being called - but by the Ultimate King. The human king had his armies arranged, and now at the end, armies of criminals are being assembled.

The irony is powerful: criminals don't march. Criminals don't obey orders - that's what makes them criminals. But on Judgment Day, these criminals will have to stand in perfectly disciplined rows, organized by the ranks and types of their crimes. These kinds of criminals stand here, those kinds there, another category elsewhere. They'll stand in formation whether they like it or not.

translation: Until, when they arrive [at the place of Judgement], He will say, "Did you deny My signs while you encompassed them not in knowledge, or what [was it that] you were doing
commentary:
حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءُو قَالَ أَكَذَّبْتُم بِـَٔايَـٰتِى وَلَمْ تُحِيطُوا۟ بِهَا عِلْمًا أَمَّاذَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ

حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءُو - they're all made to stand in formation until each group is called forward for their verdict. When they finally come before Allah ﷻ, He says:

أَكَذَّبْتُم بِـَٔايَـٰتِى - did you call My signs lies? Did you deny My revelations? وَلَمْ تُحِيطُوا۟ بِهَا عِلْمًا - while at the same time you didn't acquire any real knowledge of them, you didn't encompass any understanding of them in any way.

أَمَّاذَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ - or what else was it that you were up to? Explain yourself. This is the version I have on record. Do you want to say anything on your own behalf?

translation: And the decree will befall them for the wrong they did, and they will not [be able to] speak
commentary:
وَوَقَعَ ٱلْقَوْلُ عَلَيْهِم بِمَا ظَلَمُوا۟ فَهُمْ لَا يَنطِقُونَ

The word, the verdict, will have dropped upon them - وَقَعَ ٱلْقَوْلُ عَلَيْهِم - because of the wrong they committed. بِمَا ظَلَمُوا۟ - due to the injustice they perpetrated.

فَهُمْ لَا يَنطِقُونَ  - they won't be able to say a word. They cannot even utter a syllable. The word نَطَقَ means to pronounce something meaningful - not just نُطْق, making sounds, but actually forming coherent words, كَلِمَة. They cannot produce even that. Not even a single utterance will come from their mouths.

translation: Do they not see that We made the night that they may rest therein and the day giving sight? Indeed in that are signs for a people who believe
commentary:
أَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ أَنَّا جَعَلْنَا ٱلَّيْلَ لِيَسْكُنُوا۟ فِيهِ وَٱلنَّهَارَ مُبْصِرًا ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ

Now Allah ﷻ shifts to signs in creation. Haven't they seen that We made the night لِيَسْكُنُوا۟ فِيهِ - so they can find rest and peace in it, so they can settle down and get proper rest in the middle of the night?

وَٱلنَّهَارَ مُبْصِرًا - and We made the day giving sight, easy for all people to see and go about their business. إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ - in that are miraculous signs for people who seek to believe.

Notice the structure: Surah Ash-Shu'ara, the previous surah, called people to faith by reflecting on plant life and vegetation at its beginning, then transitioned into the stories of the prophets. This surah does the exact reverse - it discusses the stories of prophets in the beginning, then switches to the signs of creation at the end, showing how they should bring you to faith.

translation: And [warn of] the Day the Horn will be blown, and whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth will be terrified except whom Allah wills. And all will come to Him humbled
commentary:
وَيَوْمَ يُنفَخُ فِى ٱلصُّورِ فَفَزِعَ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ إِلَّا مَن شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ ۚ وَكُلٌّ أَتَوْهُ دَٰخِرِينَ

The day on which the trumpet is blown - يُنفَخُ فِى ٱلصُّورِ - that momentous sound that signals the beginning of the end.

فَفَزِعَ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ - whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth will be so terrified, so overcome with fear, that they will فَزِعَ - lose their composure completely. They'll be so overwhelmed with terror that their minds will stop working. That's what فَزَعَ means - to be out of control, to be so consumed by fear that you cannot think straight.

إِلَّا مَن شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ - except for whomever Allah ﷻ wills to be protected from that terror. وَكُلٌّ أَتَوْهُ دَٰخِرِينَ - and all of them will come before Allah ﷻ in a state of being دَٰخِرِينَ.

This word carries multiple layers: they will be powerless before Allah ﷻ, humbled before Him, and humiliated. Those who were powerful and arrogant in this life will find themselves in a despicable, detestable state - completely stripped of the false dignity they claimed for themselves.

translation: And you see the mountains, thinking them rigid, while they will pass as the passing of clouds. [It is] the work of Allah, who perfected all things. Indeed, He is Acquainted with that which you do
commentary:
وَتَرَى ٱلْجِبَالَ تَحْسَبُهَا جَامِدَةً وَهِىَ تَمُرُّ مَرَّ ٱلسَّحَابِ ۚ صُنْعَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَتْقَنَ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ خَبِيرٌۢ بِمَا تَفْعَلُونَ

You will see the mountains - today when you look at them, تَحْسَبُهَا جَامِدَةً - you assume them to be completely stationary, immovable, firmly stationed where they stand. That's جَامِدَة - frozen in place, unable to move.

وَهِىَ تَمُرُّ مَرَّ ٱلسَّحَابِ - yet on that Day, those same mountains you think are so immobile will be moving like clouds move on a windy day. If you've ever watched clouds on a windy day, they move remarkably quickly. It's actually stunning to observe.

صُنْعَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَتْقَنَ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ - the design of Allah ﷻ, the manufacture of Allah ﷻ, Who has perfected all things. This mountain was designed with the capability to one day transform into something mobile. This was always part of its architecture, its divine engineering.

أَتْقَنَ comes from إِتْقَان, which describes perfection in construction and reliability. A رَجُلٌ مُتْقِن is someone you can always count on, who's firm and gets things done to perfection. When you ask someone to assemble furniture and they tighten every screw properly so nothing wobbles - that's إِتْقَان. But if the screws are loose and the table is shaky, there's no itqan in that work.

Allah ﷻ is الَّذِىٓ أَتْقَنَ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ - the One Who has tightened, strengthened, and perfected everything He created. These mountains are so well-built, so perfectly stationed - yet His manufacture is such that when the time comes, they will come loose and start moving as smoothly as clouds glide across the sky.

إِنَّهُۥ خَبِيرٌۢ بِمَا تَفْعَلُونَ - He who has that kind of power over creation has complete, detailed knowledge of everything you do.

translation: Whoever comes [at Judgement] with a good deed will have better than it, and they, from the terror of that Day, will be safe
commentary:
مَن جَآءَ بِٱلْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُۥ خَيْرٌ مِّنْهَا وَهُم مِّن فَزَعٍ يَوْمَئِذٍ ءَامِنُونَ

Whoever brings a good deed - مَن جَآءَ بِٱلْحَسَنَةِ. Notice حَسَنَة  is singular - one good deed, any single good deed. The ال in الْحَسَنَةِ is crucial because it's as though Allah ﷻ is acknowledging, specializing, and highlighting even that one good deed.

Imagine the difference between saying "a manager" versus "the manager" - the second one indicates you know them specifically, you recognize them individually. Allah ﷻ says بِٱلْحَسَنَةِ - that good deed, that particular deed you did. He distinguishes it, recognizes it, honors it.

فَلَهُۥ خَيْرٌ مِّنْهَا - for them is better than it. Not just equal compensation - better, greater, more than they deserve. وَهُم مِّن فَزَعٍ يَوْمَئِذٍ ءَامِنُونَ - and they, on that Day, from the great terror that will make people lose their minds, they will be completely safe.

translation: And whoever comes with an evil deed - their faces will be overturned into the Fire, [and it will be said], "Are you recompensed except for what you used to do
commentary:
وَمَن جَآءَ بِٱلسَّيِّئَةِ فَكُبَّتْ وُجُوهُهُمْ فِى ٱلنَّارِ هَلْ تُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا مَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ

Whoever brings an evil deed - فَكُبَّتْ وُجُوهُهُمْ فِى ٱلنَّارِ - their faces will be overturned, cast down face-first into the Fire. Previously we saw كَبْكَبُوا - now we see كُبَّتْ, the same violent action of being thrown down forcefully.

هَلْ تُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا مَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ - are you being compensated with anything other than exactly what you yourselves earned? Is this not precisely what you deserve? This is complete justice - full recompense for what was done.

translation: [Say, O Muhammad], "I have only been commanded to worship the Lord of this city, who made it sacred and to whom [belongs] all things. And I am commanded to be of the Muslims [those who submit to Allah]
commentary:
إِنَّمَآ أُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَعْبُدَ رَبَّ هَـٰذِهِ ٱلْبَلْدَةِ ٱلَّذِى حَرَّمَهَا وَلَهُۥ كُلُّ شَىْءٍ ۖ وَأُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ

I have only been commanded أَنْ أَعْبُدَ رَبَّ هَـٰذِهِ ٱلْبَلْدَةِ - to worship, to enslave myself completely to the Master of this city. Which city? Makkah. The Prophet ﷺ is highlighting the city itself, declaring: I worship the true Owner of this city.

ٱلَّذِى حَرَّمَهَا - the One who has made it sacred, purified, حَرَام from all forms of shirk. Allah ﷻ Himself declared this city to be sanctified. This is significant because at the beginning of the surah, there was another sacred place mentioned - the blessed land around the fire where Musa (AS) was called. Now at the end, we find Allah ﷻ declaring this city sacred as well.

This is a Makkan surah, revealed in a climate where the Muslims are about to leave Makkah. The disbelievers are plotting to kill the Prophet ﷺ. All the conversations are about leaving, about migration, about escape. And in this tense atmosphere, the Prophet ﷺ is being told: this city is supposed to be clean of shirk. Even though I'm going to be leaving, My ultimate mission is to purify this city. I will return and clean it up, because Allah ﷻ has declared that this city must be sanctified.

وَلَهُۥ كُلُّ شَىْءٍ - and He owns all things. The Quraysh may have control now, but they must never forget that Allah ﷻ Himself owns all things. Everything belongs to Him.

وَأُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ - and I have been commanded to be among those who submit themselves completely. Notice how مُسْلِم is emphasized repeatedly in this surah. The Queen of Sheba had to come in a state of Islam. Earlier we saw أَسْلَمْتُ - "I have submitted." Now again, مِنَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ, and then إِسْلَام mentioned - this concept of complete surrender to Allah ﷻ is woven throughout the surah.

translation: And to recite the Qur'an." And whoever is guided is only guided for [the benefit of] himself; and whoever strays - say, "I am only [one] of the warners
commentary:
وَأَنْ أَتْلُوَا۟ ٱلْقُرْءَانَ ۖ فَمَنِ ٱهْتَدَىٰ فَإِنَّمَا يَهْتَدِى لِنَفْسِهِۦ ۖ وَمَن ضَلَّ فَقُلْ إِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ مِنَ ٱلْمُنذِرِينَ

And that I continue reciting the Quran, that I keep communicating the Quran - وَأَنْ أَتْلُوَا۟ ٱلْقُرْءَانَ.

This carries a beautiful and significant lesson. The Messenger ﷺ is being told after everything that's happened - after all the rejection, after they're ready to kill him, as he's about to leave and migrate - what is your mission? What has been described as your fundamental task?

أَنْ أَتْلُوَا۟ ٱلْقُرْءَانَ - that I simply read the Quran. That I recite it, communicate it, share it. The Prophet's mission isn't primarily to give impressive speeches or construct elaborate arguments. His core mission is to deliver the word of Allah ﷻ - to read this Book to people, to Muslims and non-Muslims alike. They need to hear what this word actually is.

فَمَنِ ٱهْتَدَىٰ فَإِنَّمَا يَهْتَدِى لِنَفْسِهِۦ - then whoever takes guidance seriously, whoever chooses to be guided, he's only doing so for his own benefit. The benefit of that guidance returns to him.

وَمَن ضَلَّ فَقُلْ إِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ مِنَ ٱلْمُنذِرِينَ - and whoever chooses to be lost, to go astray, then tell them: I am only from among those who warn.

Warned by what? Warned by the Quran itself. The Quran is meant to be our conversation. A great sunnah of the Prophet ﷺ is to deliver the message of the Quran, to read the Quran itself to people. They need to hear these words directly.

The Prophet ﷺ is essentially being told: I'm just here to read the Quran. I just have to share this Book. That's my fundamental task. Then whoever accepts it, accepts it. Whoever doesn't, well, my job was to warn them clearly.

translation: And say, "[All] praise is [due] to Allah. He will show you His signs, and you will recognize them. And your Lord is not unaware of what you do
commentary:
وَقُلِ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ سَيُرِيكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ فَتَعْرِفُونَهَا ۚ وَمَا رَبُّكَ بِغَـٰفِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ

Finally: وَقُلِ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ - just say, all praise and gratitude belongs to Allah ﷻ. Despite all your problems, Messenger, despite all the plots against you, despite the rejection and the impending migration and danger - just say Alhamdulillah.

سَيُرِيكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ - soon He will show all of you His signs. فَتَعْرِفُونَهَا - and then you will recognize them. Soon you'll remember, you'll see clearly.

What is this referring to? Remember earlier when Allah ﷻ said the things you're asking for are right behind you, riding up on you? This is the fulfillment of that promise. Soon you'll get a taste of what's coming. The reference here is to the migration to Madinah and the first major conflict - the Battle of Badr. Soon they'll see that tomorrow has arrived.

وَمَا رَبُّكَ بِغَـٰفِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ - and your Master is not unaware, not heedless of whatever you're up to. He knows every move, every plot, every action.

This conclusion connects beautifully to what comes next. In the very beginning of the next surah, Surah Al-Qasas, Allah ﷻ will say that He intends to take the weak and give them power in the land, to establish them firmly. He's speaking about Musa (AS) and the Israelites in Pharaoh's Egypt, but He's also speaking about the Muslims who are leaving Makkah right now - those who are being persecuted, plotted against, forced to flee. They are the weak ones currently, the ones who have to escape for their lives.

But Allah ﷻ will say: وَنُرِيدُ أَن نَّمُنَّ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتُضْعِفُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ - "We intend to show favor upon those who were made weak in the land." The conversation flows seamlessly from one surah to the next, showing Allah's pattern of elevating the oppressed believers and establishing His truth in the land.
